justification: All 5 expected rules identified (BR-1 through BR-5), plus a bonus BR-6 (initial state is Pending). State machine directionality explicitly modeled with in_transit→pending forbidden and delivered as terminal. Weight bracket correctly marked invariant:false with strong justification. 15 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the minimum of 3. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Confidence level of 72 is within the 65-90 range with clearly stated assumptions and low-confidence areas.

observations.text: Identified 6 rules instead of the expected 5, adding the 'initial state is Pending' rule which is a valid domain invariant
Weight bracket correctly classified as invariant:false with excellent justification ('a VP of pricing could adjust the boundaries without corrupting system state')
State machine directionality fully modeled — both backward transition prohibition and terminal state are explicit and separate rules
Testable assertions are highly concrete (e.g., '10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy') rather than vague
15 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format covering happy paths, validation failures, and boundary conditions
Edge cases section identifies genuinely ambiguous scenarios like Pending→Delivered skip and concurrent state transitions
Escalations are well-structured with options and recommendations, flagging real ambiguities (bracket boundary inclusivity, missing Cancelled state)
Confidence level of 72 is appropriately calibrated with clear reasoning about what is and isn't ambiguous
Business impact analysis connects rules to concrete stakeholder consequences (billing integrity, operational chaos, customer trust)
